scope: Pass B full-surface expansion over the 4 legacy-parity CRM objects (customers, contacts, leads, opportunities) this connector already reads: every publicly documented action (getAll/get/create/update/delete/search/config) for each of the 4 objects is enumerated below. Apptivo's DAO API is uniform across ~30+ app objects platform-wide (cases, invoices, projects, employees, etc.) but this bundle stays scoped to the 4 legacy-parity CRM objects per docs/migration/conventions.md's per-connector (not per-platform) surface-review unit; the other Apptivo apps are a distinct, larger migration a future capability-expansion pass would size separately.

excluded.reason: ENGINE_GAP: createCustomer's real wire contract is a single query parameter (customerData) whose value is the ENTIRE record JSON-serialized as one string (https://www.apptivo.com/developer-api/customers-api-reference/createcustomer/ — 'a=save&customerData={CUSTOMER_DATA}'), not per-field query/form parameters. writes.json's WriteAction has no query field at all (only path/path_fields/body_type/body_fields), and no interpolation filter in this dialect serializes an entire record object to a JSON string for embedding in a path/query template (urlencode/unix_seconds/base64/join/last_path_segment/const all operate on a single scalar value) — expressing this write would need either a query field added to WriteAction plus a to_json-style filter, or a WriteHook (a 3rd-hook-interface Tier-2 escalation for a single action, out of proportion here). Documented as a genuine engine gap, not silently worked around; the analogous updateCustomer/createLead/updateLead/createOpportunity/updateOpportunity/createContacts/updateContact actions below share the identical gap.

ENGINE_GAP: same whole-record-as-one-JSON-query-value contract as createCustomer above (customerData + attributeNames + objectId + customerId all as separate query params, https://www.apptivo.com/developer-api/customers-api-reference/updatecustomer/), plus an attributeNames array param this bundle would need to derive from which fields changed — no such 'which fields were modified' concept exists in the write dialect's per-record model either. See createCustomer's excluded entry for the shared root cause.

Returns the account's CRM object schema/configuration metadata (custom field definitions, picklist values), not customer domain records — a schema-introspection endpoint, matching the api_surface convention's non_data_endpoint category (see stripe's /v1/balance precedent).
Email-address search over the same customer records the customers stream (getAll) already reads in full; a filtered/narrower view of already-covered data, not new data.
Free-text search over the same customer records the customers stream already reads in full; same reasoning as advancedSearchCustomers.

ENGINE_GAP: createContacts shares the identical whole-record-as-one-JSON-query-value contract as customers' createCustomer (contactData query param carrying the full serialized record) — see that entry's reason for the shared root cause.
ENGINE_GAP: updateContact shares the identical contract as customers' updateCustomer (contactData + attributeNames + objectId + contactId) — see that entry's reason.

Apptivo does not publish a documented deleteContact DAO API reference page (unlike customers, which has an explicit https://www.apptivo.com/developer-api/customers-api-reference/deletecustomer/ page) — end-user docs only describe deleting a contact through the web UI (https://www.apptivo.com/contacts-app-documentation/contact-management/delete-contact/), not a DAO API action. Not implemented without a confirmed action name/param contract; would need vendor confirmation or a live account trace, not something to guess at for a mutating/irreversible action.
Schema/configuration metadata endpoint, not contact domain records — same reasoning as customers' getCustomersConfigData.
Search over the same contact records the contacts stream already reads in full.

ENGINE_GAP: createLead (createLeadWithLeadSource) shares the identical whole-record-as-one-JSON-query-value contract (leadData query param) — see customers' createCustomer entry for the shared root cause.
ENGINE_GAP: updateLead shares the identical contract as customers' updateCustomer (leadData + attributeNames + objectId + leadId) — see that entry's reason.

Apptivo does not publish a documented deleteLead DAO API reference page (the public Leads API Reference index lists only getLeadsConfigData/createLead/updateLead/search-by-email) — not implemented without a confirmed action/param contract for the same reason as contacts' delete.
Schema/configuration metadata endpoint, not lead domain records.
Email-address search over the same lead records the leads stream already reads in full.
ENGINE_GAP: createOpportunity shares the identical whole-record-as-one-JSON-query-value contract (opportunityData query param) — see customers' createCustomer entry for the shared root cause.
ENGINE_GAP: updateOpportunity shares the identical contract as customers' updateCustomer (opportunityData + attributeNames + objectId + opportunityId) — see that entry's reason.

Apptivo does not publish a documented deleteOpportunity DAO API reference page (the public Opportunities API Reference index lists only getOpportunitiesConfigData/createOpportunity/updateOpportunity/searchOpportunities) — not implemented without a confirmed action/param contract for the same reason as contacts' and leads' delete.
Schema/configuration metadata endpoint, not opportunity domain records.
Email-address search over the same opportunity records the opportunities stream already reads in full.

Apptivo's DAO v6 API is uniform across ~30+ additional app objects beyond the 4 CRM objects this connector covers (cases, invoices, estimates, orders, projects, employees, items, targets, jobs, events, testimonials, and more per https://www.apptivo.com/api-reference/) — each follows the identical getAll/save/update/delete pattern already modeled for customers/contacts/leads/opportunities. Scoped out of this Pass B pass as a platform-wide expansion (a distinct, much larger migration) rather than this connector's legacy-parity CRM object set; see this bundle's scope note.
